brand.name: W.A.Y.
brand.slogan: Discover who you are. Shape who you become.
brand.short: A calm digital space for career clarity, self-discovery, and the next step.

media.heroAlt: Students working together in a modern learning space.
media.profileAlt: Students walking through a bright campus hallway.
media.guideAlt: A laptop in a quiet study environment.

home.headline: Where your questions become direction.
home.support: W.A.Y. helps students understand interests, strengths, working style, and possible career directions without pressure or noise.
home.visualTitle: A clearer next step starts with one honest answer.
home.visualBody: Take a guided test, reflect with W.A.Y. Guide, and turn uncertainty into a personal roadmap.
home.howTitle: A calm flow from reflection to action.
home.howLead: Focused, supportive, and useful from the first question.

steps.oneBody: Explore interests, strengths, motivation, environment, and communication style.
steps.twoBody: See recommended directions and career matches with clear reasons.
steps.threeBody: Save results, compare professions, and talk through ideas with W.A.Y. Guide.

home.whyLead: Career choice becomes discovery. Results are guidance, not a label.

benefits.clarityBody: Supportive copy and focused flows help students reflect honestly.
benefits.depthBody: Cards explain what roles do, who they suit, and how to start.
benefits.futureBody: Prepared for backend services, profiles, and AI integration.

home.audienceTitle: For students, parents, and mentors looking for a better conversation.
home.audienceLead: W.A.Y. creates shared language around strengths, options, and practical first steps.
home.ctaTitle: Start with curiosity. Leave with direction.
home.ctaBody: The first test takes only a few minutes and gives you a grounded starting point.

about.title: A portal for students learning to name their future.
about.lead: W.A.Y. exists because choosing a direction can feel heavy before you have the words for who you are becoming.
about.missionBody: Help school students make thoughtful education and career choices through reflection, structured guidance, and emotionally intelligent product design.
about.problemBody: Advice is often too generic, too late, or too focused on prestige. W.A.Y. starts earlier and makes the process personal.
about.helpBody: It connects test insights, profession exploration, guide conversations, and saved roadmaps in one clear experience.
about.teamLead: The team section is ready for real contributors as the product grows.

onboarding.title: This is a guide, not a life sentence.
onboarding.lead: The test identifies patterns in your interests, strengths, and preferred environments.
onboarding.durationBody: About 7-10 minutes, with progress saved as you move.
onboarding.resultBody: You will receive a profile summary, recommended directions, career matches, and a practical next-step roadmap.
onboarding.noteBody: No result can define you completely. Use it as guidance, then keep exploring.

test.title: Answer honestly. There are no perfect choices.

questions.q1: I enjoy finding out why things work the way they do.
questions.q2: I like turning ideas into visuals, layouts, or experiences.
questions.q3: I feel energized when I help people understand something.
questions.q4: I am curious about digital tools, systems, and technology.
questions.q5: I notice opportunities to improve how a project or business works.
questions.q6: I can stay patient while comparing facts and details.
questions.q7: I often sense what another person might be feeling.
questions.q8: I remember images, shapes, and atmosphere easily.
questions.q9: I like organizing tasks into a clear plan.
questions.q10: Accuracy matters to me, even when the task takes longer.
questions.q11: I enjoy working independently when I need deep focus.
questions.q12: I do my best work when ideas are discussed with a team.
questions.q13: I like situations where decisions need to be made quickly.
questions.q14: I can spend a long time improving one complex solution.
questions.q15: I feel calm when expectations and steps are clear.
questions.q16: I can explain an idea confidently to a group.
questions.q17: I listen carefully before giving advice.
questions.q18: I enjoy writing, storytelling, or shaping a message.
questions.q19: I can find common ground between different opinions.
questions.q20: I like teaching someone a skill or topic.
questions.q21: Solving logical puzzles feels satisfying to me.
questions.q22: I often imagine different ways something could look or feel.
questions.q23: I like testing ideas through experiments.
questions.q24: I enjoy thinking about long-term strategy.
questions.q25: I care about the emotional effect of a story or product.
questions.q26: A creative studio or project room sounds appealing.
questions.q27: A lab, research room, or precise workspace sounds appealing.
questions.q28: A digital product team sounds appealing.
questions.q30: A business, market, or startup environment sounds appealing.
questions.q31: I want my work to make life better for other people.
questions.q32: I feel motivated when I build something useful.
questions.q33: I feel motivated by discovering new knowledge.
questions.q34: I feel motivated by expressing an original idea.
questions.q35: I feel motivated by growth, leadership, and responsibility.

results.title: You are starting to see the pattern.
results.summary: Your answers suggest analytical curiosity, visual thinking, and care for how people experience ideas.
results.explanationBody: Treat these matches as invitations to explore. A high match is not a fixed future.
results.emptyLead: Complete the test to create your first saved direction profile.
results.workStyle: You seem to prefer focused work with room for thoughtful collaboration and visible progress.
results.environment: You may enjoy modern learning, product, research, or studio environments.

step1.title: Pick two professions to investigate
step1.description: Write down what feels exciting, unclear, and challenging.

step2.title: Try a small project
step2.description: Build a tiny portfolio piece, interview someone, or explain a concept.

step3.title: Reflect with a mentor
step3.description: Bring your result to a teacher, parent, or W.A.Y. Guide conversation.

reasons.uxDesigner: Your answers point to empathy, research, and visual problem solving.
reasons.frontendDeveloper: Your profile connects logic, digital tools, and visible experiences.
reasons.dataAnalyst: Your pattern-focused answers match analytical work.
reasons.researchScientist: Your curiosity and patience fit careful investigation.
reasons.productManager: Your planning and communication strengths support product direction.
reasons.psychologistEducator: Your empathy and listening patterns suit support roles.
reasons.teacherMentor: Your teaching and communication signals are strong.
reasons.medicalSpecialist: Your precision and care fit health-related exploration.
reasons.entrepreneur: Your motivation around growth and action fits business building.

professions.title: Compare roles without the pressure to choose today.
professions.lead: Each profession includes what it does, who it suits, and first steps.
professions.searchPlaceholder: Search by role, skill, or category

uxDesigner.title: UX Designer
uxDesigner.description: Designs digital experiences around real human needs.
uxDesigner.does: Researches needs, maps journeys, creates prototypes, and improves products.
uxDesigner.suits: Students who enjoy empathy, visual structure, and practical problems.

frontendDeveloper.title: Frontend Developer
frontendDeveloper.description: Builds the visible parts of websites and apps.
frontendDeveloper.does: Turns designs and ideas into interactive interfaces with code.
frontendDeveloper.suits: Students who like logic, visual results, and improving details.

dataAnalyst.title: Data Analyst
dataAnalyst.description: Finds patterns in information and explains what they mean.
dataAnalyst.does: Collects, cleans, compares, and visualizes data to support decisions.
dataAnalyst.suits: Students who enjoy patterns, evidence, and clear explanations.

researchScientist.title: Research Scientist
researchScientist.description: Investigates questions through study and experiments.
researchScientist.does: Builds hypotheses, tests ideas, reads research, and shares findings.
researchScientist.suits: Students who like depth, patience, accuracy, and discovery.

productManager.title: Product Manager
productManager.description: Connects user needs, business goals, and team execution.
productManager.does: Sets direction, prioritizes features, studies users, and aligns teams.
productManager.suits: Students who like strategy, communication, and decisions.

psychologistEducator.title: Psychologist Educator
psychologistEducator.description: Supports learning, reflection, and healthy growth.
psychologistEducator.does: Helps people understand behavior, emotions, and learning patterns.
psychologistEducator.suits: Students who are empathetic, patient, and careful with trust.

teacherMentor.title: Teacher or Mentor
teacherMentor.description: Helps others understand ideas and grow confidence.
teacherMentor.does: Explains topics, designs learning moments, and supports progress.
teacherMentor.suits: Students who enjoy helping, explaining, and encouraging others.

medicalSpecialist.title: Medical Specialist
medicalSpecialist.description: Uses science and care to protect people's health.
medicalSpecialist.does: Studies symptoms, applies medical knowledge, and supports patients.
medicalSpecialist.suits: Students who value precision, resilience, and responsibility.

entrepreneur.title: Entrepreneur
entrepreneur.description: Creates solutions, tests ideas, and builds projects.
entrepreneur.does: Finds problems, experiments with solutions, attracts support, and adapts.
entrepreneur.suits: Students who like ownership, initiative, and learning through action.

steps.interviewPeople: Interview three people about an app.
steps.redesignApp: Redesign one screen from an app you use often.
steps.cloneUi: Recreate a simple interface for practice.
steps.presentChart: Explain one chart to a friend.
steps.readPaper: Read a short science article and summarize it.
steps.joinOlympiad: Join a science club or olympiad group.
steps.runExperiment: Run a simple experiment and record results.
steps.studyProducts: Study why a favorite product works well.
steps.planFeature: Write a one-page plan for a new feature.
steps.volunteer: Volunteer in a learning or support environment.
steps.tutorFriend: Tutor a classmate in one topic.
steps.presentTopic: Present a topic clearly to a small group.
steps.shadowProfessional: Interview or shadow a health professional.
steps.findProblem: Find one problem people around you face.
steps.testIdea: Test a small solution with five people.

guide.title: A reflective guide for career questions.
guide.lead: A real AI-backed guidance space for career reflection. It is not therapy or a replacement for trusted support.
guide.loginRequired: Sign in to keep W.A.Y. Guide conversations saved with your profile.

messages.welcome: Tell me what feels unclear. We can turn it into one small next question.
messages.lostReply: Feeling lost usually means there are too many paths at once. Start by naming one thing you want more of.

responses.reflect: Try separating what you enjoy from what you feel pressured to choose.
responses.results: Your result is a map of patterns, not a verdict. Pick one recommended profession and explore it.
responses.choice: A wrong choice is less scary when you treat it as an experiment.
responses.plan: Choose two roles, learn one basic skill for each, talk to one person, then compare how each path feels.

profile.title: Your saved direction space.
profile.demoNote: Your saved profile, results, professions, chats, and roadmap.
profile.loginRequired: Sign in to see your saved results, professions, and W.A.Y. Guide conversations.
profile.themePlaceholder: Dark theme active. More themes later.
profile.notificationsPlaceholder: Notification preferences will connect to the backend later.

auth.loginLead: Continue your W.A.Y. profile with a secure account.
auth.signupLead: Start saving results, professions, and guide reflections.
auth.name: Name
auth.toLogin: Already have an account? Log in
auth.mockNotice: Your password is sent only to the W.A.Y. API and stored as a secure hash.

validation.password: Password must be at least 6 characters.
validation.passwordLong: Password must be at least 8 characters.
validation.name: Enter your name.

notFound.title: This path is not on the map yet.
notFound.lead: Return to W.A.Y. and keep exploring from a clearer starting point.

admin.title: W.A.Y. operations dashboard.
admin.lead: Monitor usage, support students, and manage accounts from one protected space.
admin.loginRequired: Sign in with an admin account to open the dashboard.
admin.forbiddenLead: Your current account does not have admin permissions.
